insight: In Prismata's SDR (Strictly Dominated by Replacement) framework, 'improved statuses' are strictly defined and do not include Prompt or buildtime for normal purchases. Exhaustedness is considered a strictly worse status, while Blocker (independent of promptness) dominates non-Blocker on the same unit — you should never wish your unit lacked Blocker.
author: consensus
context: Extended expert discussion about what constitutes an 'improved status' for SDR purposes, with namington, http404error, liadahlia, mrguy888, and .bky_1556 all participating.

insight: SDR (Strictly Dominated by Replacement) is broken when a player might wish their units had less HP, that they didn't have a unit at all, that they had less resources/attack/supply seeds, that a unit lacked Blocker, or that a unit had lower lifespan/stamina. It is NOT broken by wishing for less buildtime, no invulnerability, no Prompt on a purchased unit, or having a Drone instead of a Vivid Drone, as those cases already exist in the game.
author: http404error
context: http404error summarizing conditions for breaking vs. not breaking SDR in a design challenge discussion.

insight: Prompt does not dominate gold (constructing) status, and gold does not dominate Prompt either — neither is strictly better than the other in the SDR sense, similar to how 2 Husks and 1 Wall do not dominate each other (Wall is usually better but loses value against Frostbite).
author: consensus
context: Discussion of which statuses dominate which in the SDR framework, with liadahlia, .bky_1556, and http404error contributing.

insight: Steelsplitter is typically not bought because you want Steelsplitters specifically — you go blue for Wall or other units like Manticore fuel, and buy Steelsplitters to avoid wasting blue resources. Gauss Cannon can sometimes be the better buy, and 2 Drones can be better than a Steelsplitter in some cases.
author: http404error
context: Side discussion comparing Steelsplitter and Gauss Cannon as base-set attackers, prompted by a comment about unit design.

insight: Green mana is saved (carries over between turns) while the more expensive resources (Blue, Red) are not. This creates meaningful asymmetry in opportunity costs across resource types: wasting unsaved resources is more punishing, and the saveable nature of green enables units with high green costs to exist viably.
author: consensus
context: liadahlia, http404error, and kaelii discussing the strategic implications of resource-saving mechanics in the context of unit design.

insight: A hypothetical unit called 'Berserker' (8GRR, 4 supply, 4 Health, Blocker, Fragile, Click: gain 1 attack per missing health) was assessed as effectively a cheaper, buildtime-2 Cynestra once it has taken 3 damage, making it extremely powerful and likely breaking the game by strongly punishing any early attacking (e.g., making Tarsiers nearly unplayable in the first 5 turns).
author: consensus
context: Expert analysis of a community-submitted unit design for a design challenge, with http404error and liadahlia evaluating its power level.

insight: The submitted 'Gauss Splitter' unit (6G, 10 supply, 3 Health, Blocker, Click: gain 1 attack, becomes Fragile the first time it absorbs damage) was confirmed by http404error and .bky_1556 to genuinely break SDR due to a very obscure game rule, with http404error noting the rule likely exists solely because of SDR.
author: http404error
context: .bky_1556 posed this as a puzzle on Reddit and in Discord; http404error confirmed it as a valid SDR-breaking unit after initially being skeptical.

insight: A Wall absorbs roughly 8 health over 4 turns (2 absorb per turn), and a Matrix (Defense Grid) absorbs 24 health over 6 turns plus its own health. These benchmarks were used by liadahlia to evaluate the relative value of hypothetical defensive unit designs involving granular blocking.
author: liadahlia
context: liadahlia performing rough cost-benefit analysis on a custom unit design by comparing to known defensive units.

insight: In Prismata, all Green+Red tech units with more than 1 health are Fragile, with the notable exception of Endotherm Kit. Antima Comet has GR cost and 1 health. This is an observed pattern in the base unit set rather than a codified rule.
author: liadahlia
context: Discussion about patterns in GR unit design and whether 1-health Fragile units exist in the game.

insight: SDR breaking can be framed as: 'Take something that is currently Always True in Prismata and make it no longer Always True.' For example, it is currently Always True that a player would rather lose a Wall than a Steelsplitter, but a unit that required sacrificing a Wall to build would invalidate that. This framing exposes meaningful gray areas in SDR definitions.
author: http404error
context: http404error synthesizing the SDR design challenge discussion with an alternative framing of what it means to break SDR.
